content: IIUC from the discussion in the last call, we lack a uniform way of rewriting all the imports once @Miguel Ojeda 's changes to rustfmt rules land. I wonder if Coccinelle can be used for that? It seems to be getting really powerful from what I recall from Roy's presentation at Kangrejos.

I've noticed that Cursor (an AI editor that is a fork of VS Code) is easier, faster, and more capable that Coccinelle for this kind of task.

I've used it for very similar things in the kernel, such as finding all the "?" return cases and changing them (for debug sessions).

When rustfmt supports what we need, then at that point we only need to remove the comments and reapply it. If you mean meanwhile, then yeah, those ideas are good for maintainers that want to already align the style for one reason or another (although I would wait until I have full confirmation that style will be supported exactly).

For now, what I am suggesting is that, if one needs to add new import blocks or change existing ones in a way that rustfmt changes the whole block anyway, then yes, please apply the new style, since it is getting changed regardless.
